items.content: JSTOR: Used extensively for accessing academic papers and research articles, particularly for literature reviews and background research on AI ethics and ML fairness. It's a primary source for scholarly work.

Congressional Research Service (CRS) Reports: Michael frequently references CRS reports when preparing testimony or policy recommendations on AI regulation. These reports offer non-partisan analysis crucial for his legislative engagements.

GitHub: Essential for accessing and contributing to open-source ML fairness toolkits and code repositories. He uses it for both personal research projects and to share code with the non-profit he co-founded.

National Park Service (NPS) Website: Used for planning hiking trips in the Sierra Nevada, checking trail conditions, and understanding park regulations. It's a key resource for his outdoor recreation.
Local Chamber Music Society Mailing List: Subscribed to receive updates on performances and events for his cello quartet. This helps him coordinate rehearsals and performances.
Brewfather App: A popular homebrewing application used to design recipes, track fermentation, and log batches of his Taiwanese-style craft beer. It helps him manage and refine his brewing process.
